description:
You are the on–call backup-verification engineer.  
  A recent backup image has been deposited at the following absolute path:

  /home/user/backups/weekly_backup.img

  Your job is to confirm that the file is un-altered by recording its SHA-256 digest.

  What you must deliver
  =====================
  1. A new regular file called
     /home/user/logs/weekly_backup.sha256sum

     • If the directory “/home/user/logs” does not exist, create it first.  
     • The file must contain exactly ONE line, produced by a *single* terminal
       command (pipeline or subshell is fine, but must be entered as one shell
       line).

  2. Line-format requirements (must match **exactly**; automated tests perform a
     strict string comparison):

        <64-character-lower-case-hexadecimal-digest><two spaces><absolute path to the image><newline>

     In other words, it must be the verbatim output that the standard utility
     “sha256sum” prints when it is pointed at the backup image.  There must be

        • 64 contiguous lower-case hex characters  
        • exactly TWO (2) space characters  
        • the literal text “/home/user/backups/weekly_backup.img”  
        • one UNIX newline “\n” and **nothing else** (no extra spaces,
          carriage-returns, prompt symbols, timestamps, etc.).

     Example layout (hash shown here is NOT the real one):

        da39a3ee5e6b4b0d3255bfef95601890afd80709  /home/user/backups/weekly_backup.img

  3. You may display whatever you like in the terminal while working, but the
     log file itself must obey the exact format above so that the automated
     checker can verify it.

  Success criteria
  ================
  • /home/user/logs/weekly_backup.sha256sum exists.  
  • It is world-readable (default 0644 is fine).  
  • Its single line contains the correct SHA-256 digest of the backup image in
    the precise format described above.

  Reminder
  • Operate entirely as the normal “user”; you have no sudo privileges.  
  • Complete the whole task using one compound shell line (pipe, command
    substitution, redirection, etc.) rather than multiple separate commands.  
  • Do not hand-edit the log file; generate it straight from the checksum
    command so we can trust it.
truth:
Initial state supplied to the agent
  ----------------------------------
  1. Directory: /home/user/backups  (permissions 0755)  
  2. File: /home/user/backups/weekly_backup.img  
     • Size: 0 bytes (empty file)  
     • SHA-256 digest (empty file):  
       e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

  3. Directory /home/user/logs does *not* exist when the scenario begins.

  Expected final artefacts
  ------------------------
  • Directory /home/user/logs created (0755 or stricter).  
  • File /home/user/logs/weekly_backup.sha256sum created (0644).  
  • File content (exactly 73 bytes including newline):

        e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  /home/user/backups/weekly_backup.img\n

  The test harness will:
  1. Check that the file exists.  
  2. Read its sole line and compare it byte-for-byte with the string above.
name: task_000000_c9add584
